topic: Review R2 lineage gpt55r2-c-6: MCP server infrastructure outside search/store scope

focus: C-rest-of-server daemon IPC, reconnect/replay, path/socket handling, and fail-closed behavior

findingDetails.title: Spec-memory TCP IPC endpoint is documented and launcher-supported but the server resolver rewrites it into a filesystem path
findingDetails.evidence: ENV_REFERENCE documents SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR as accepting tcp://host:port; the CLI and launcher bridge preserve tcp:// endpoints, but resolveIpcSocketPath path-resolves the env value and appends daemon-ipc.sock before startIpcSocketServer can take its TCP branch.
findingDetails.recommendation: Teach resolveIpcSocketPath to return tcp:// targets unchanged, or remove TCP support from docs/launcher paths; add an integration test that starts the spec-memory backend with SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR=tcp://127.0.0.1:<port> and proves launcher/client attach succeeds.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Scope C calls out daemon lifecycle, IPC trust, socket handling, and fail-closed behavior; cited files are the spec-memory MCP server IPC path and daemon CLI bridge.

findingDetails.title: memory_save is replayed across backend recycle while the secondary-index write path is explicitly non-idempotent
findingDetails.evidence: REPLAYABLE_TOOL_NAMES includes memory_save; the adjacent proxy comment and README both document that a replay after primary insert but before secondary-index writes can append duplicate secondary-index rows because no idempotency token keys that path. Tests assert memory_save remains replayable.
findingDetails.recommendation: Either classify memory_save as unsafe/non-replayable until the save handler has a request idempotency key across primary and secondary index writes, or add a secondary-index uniqueness/dedup transaction that makes replay safe and prove it with a commit-then-die recycle regression test.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Scope C includes reconnect/respawn, daemon lifecycle races, and fail-closed behavior; cited files are the launcher session proxy, durability stress tests, and server infrastructure README.

evidence: Scope C spec lines 7-14 mapped to daemon IPC and reconnect/replay files.
Review scope packet has no checklist; iteration evidence is file:line based.

noveltyJustification: First lineage pass for C-rest-of-server identified two active P1 infrastructure risks not requiring speculative behavior beyond documented code paths and tests.
ruledOut: No P0 assigned to TCP IPC because tcp:// is opt-in configuration and current behavior is an availability/contract failure, not a default remote-exposure proof.

No P0 assigned to memory_save replay because duplicate secondary rows require a backend recycle in a commit-then-die window; impact is data integrity and retrieval correctness risk, not deterministic loss on every save.
